name: Bangja Gui (Barbecued Beef in a Lettuce Wrapper)
recipeIngredient: 4 Scallions; white part only, cut into 2-inch long julienne strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the steak into thin slices that are 3 inches long and 2 inches wide. If the steak is partially frozen, this will be easier to do. Combine the steak with salt, 1 tablespoon of sesame oil, and black pepper. Season the meat well, then let it sit for 15 minutes.
2. Cut the scallion slices into 2-inch lengths and soak them in ice water for 15 minutes. Drain the scallions thoroughly. Mix together the soy sauce and remaining 2 teaspoons of sesame oil with the scallions. Set aside.
3. Combine the hot red chili powder, sugar, and toasted sesame seeds. Rub this mixture onto the inside curl of the lettuce leaves. Set aside.
4. Grill the steak for 1 to 3 minutes for rare, medium, or well done, depending on preference, over charcoal, gas, or electric broiler settings. Place one lettuce leaf in a serving dish, add several slices of steak, and sprinkle with the seasoned scallions. Add a sliver or two of garlic. Fold the lettuce over the steak and enjoy. Make as many sandwiches as desired.
Serve with rice and side dishes or as an appetizer with drinks.
